note: Advisory sandbox build. The deliverable is a standalone HTML renderer for the DLM founder review sheet; its proof is (a) section/count parity against the DLM module's own render_founder_sheet_md for the SAME dict, and (b) the repo language_gate dogfood (CHECK_OK when the rendered page does not FAIL, CHECK_REJECTED when an overclaim is injected). Never a bare governance PASS.
title: DLM founder review sheet as HTML (standalone render_founder_sheet_html)

subject.note: render_founder_sheet_html(sheet_dict) reads the SAME sheet dict shape as build_founder_sheet; the DLM module is NOT edited (imported read-only only to compare against render_founder_sheet_md). Sheet generated by running run_pipeline once with OUTPUT_DIR/RECEIPTS_DIR monkeypatched to a temp dir (TH-2 pattern).

what_it_proves: render_founder_sheet_html renders the SAME sections as render_founder_sheet_md for the same dict: the five summary counts, one section per advisor cluster (with cluster_id/name/plain_english/controls/if_unanswered/options/member_ids), the founder-only facts, and the machine queue preview (first 20)
cluster section count in the HTML equals summary.advisor_clusters and equals the number of '## N. [id]' headings the md renderer emits
Lock 10: the generated HTML is written only to a local out/ path and carries a visible 'DO NOT PUSH — not for public hosting' banner
every computed metric is watermarked 'SYNTHETIC — not a guaranteed claim'; the page makes no autonomy or guaranteed-outcome claim (recommended picks render as hints only)
empty-sink vs clean is distinguished: empty options / members / founder-facts / machine-queue render an explicit UNPOPULATED badge, while a genuine 0 metric still renders as 0
DATA-DRIVEN red-capable: dropping one advisor cluster from the dict changes the rendered section count (renderer tracks the data, not a fixed template)
POSITIVE — the REAL rendered page does NOT FAIL the language_gate (public surface) -> CHECK_OK (decision=WARN on undefined title-case terms only, no OVERCLAIM/BANNED_REGISTER)

NEGATIVE — a MINIMAL MUTATION of that same artifact (flip the hints note into '100% guaranteed outcome') makes the gate FAIL on OVERCLAIM -> CHECK_REJECTED; reversing the single injection reproduces the clean artifact byte-for-byte
the pipeline run writes NOTHING into the tracked decision_language_machine_v1/output|receipts, and the dogfood writes NO receipt into the tracked language_gate/receipts dir
chess_patches_applied: parity is checked against the DLM module's OWN render_founder_sheet_md (not an authored strawman) over the SAME generated dict
the sheet is the REAL pipeline output over the 80-item fixture, with all writes redirected to a temp dir (TH-2); the tracked DLM tree is untouched
two independent red-capable proofs: a data-driven cluster-count discrimination and a language_gate overclaim dogfood; the gate policy is never relaxed to pass
dogfood runs scan/decide in-process (surface=public) so no write lands in the tracked receipts dir
verdict vocab CHECK_OK / CHECK_REJECTED, origin=sandbox-advisory / authority=none; never a bare governance PASS
locks_respected: Lock 5 append-only (DLM module + language_gate read-only; DLM/output|receipts untouched via temp redirect; all writes confined to catalog/builds/DX-7)
Lock 10 commercial/dashboard (generated HTML to local out/ only, visible DO-NOT-PUSH banner, synthetic watermark, no autonomy/outcome claim, empty fields flagged UNPOPULATED)
no live call (no network/CF/Supabase/sends; pipeline + gate run in-process with writes redirected/none)
worktree not pushed; no git write commands

results.md_html_parity: all summary counts + every cluster/fact/machine id present in both; section count matches md heading count
results.red_run_canary: FIRED (asserting CHECK_OK on the overclaim mutant raised AssertionError; exit 0 with EXPECTED->FIRED)
results.pollution_outside_build_dir: none (git status --porcelain tracked changes outside DX-7 == 0)

exit_gate_B4: DX-7 done — the DLM founder sheet renders to standalone HTML with section/count parity to the module's md renderer, dogfoods clean through the language_gate, and rejects an injected overclaim by minimal mutation.
next_action: wire render_founder_sheet_html to a real (non-fixture) backlog per engagement; consider a print/PDF variant sharing the same dict contract.
